Unmuze v0.5.1 expands the local Library, adds smarter save defaults, and removes redundant interface chrome so the active sidebar section is the location cue.

What's changed since v0.5.0:
- Upgraded History into a richer local Library with search, filters, thumbnails, source URLs, creator metadata, format and quality details, saved dates, path status, and playlist grouping.
- Added Library actions to reuse a source URL, open saved files, and handle missing file paths more clearly.
- Added platform-specific defaults for YouTube, SoundCloud, and TikTok so supported sources can use different audio/video and preset choices after inspection.
- Added playlist folder mode so playlist downloads can be saved into a sanitized folder named after the playlist.
- Added completed-download actions to open the saved file or reveal it in the file manager when available.
- Moved media tool readiness into notifications instead of keeping a persistent top-bar status chip.
- Removed duplicate active/saved metrics from the top bar because the sidebar already owns session metrics.
- Removed the sidebar theme switcher because theme selection already lives in Settings.
- Restyled native dropdown controls so options better match Unmuze's dark and light surfaces.
- Removed the desktop "Current view" header and strengthened the active sidebar section as the page location indicator.
- Expanded automated coverage around Library filtering, platform defaults, playlist folder behavior, and completed-download actions.

Installers and packages:
- Windows: use Unmuze_0.5.1_x64-setup.exe for the easiest install, or the MSI if you prefer Windows Installer packaging.
- macOS: use the DMG. The app.tar.gz and .sig files are used by the in-app updater.
- Linux: AppImage, DEB, and RPM packages are attached.

Notes:
- Unmuze still requires legally permitted, publicly accessible media. It does not bypass DRM, paywalls, login restrictions, private links, region locks, or other access controls.
- latest.json and .sig files are for signed in-app updates.
